title: Oxford Nanopore Rapid Barcoding with Normalization
author: Opentrons
description: This protocol automates steps 3-10 of the Oxford Nanopore Rapid Barcoding Kit [SQK-RBK110.96](file:///Users/parrishpayne/Downloads/rapid-barcoding-kit-96-sqk-rbk110-96-RBK_9126_v110_revO_24Mar2021-gridion%20(3).pdf). Choose between 16-32 samples per run (> 24 samples requires two 4-in-1 tube racks)
steps_raw: This protocol automates steps 3-10 of the Oxford Nanopore Rapid Barcoding Kit [SQK-RBK110.96](file:///Users/parrishpayne/Downloads/rapid-barcoding-kit-96-sqk-rbk110-96-RBK_9126_v110_revO_24Mar2021-gridion%20(3).pdf). Choose between 16-32 samples per run (> 24 samples requires two 4-in-1 tube racks)
Transfer 50 ng genomic DNA per sample. Adjust the volume to 9 μl with nuclease-free water. Mix by pipetting.
Transfer 1 µL of the Rapid Barcodes to each sample. Mix by pipetting
Incubate the plate at 30°C for 2 minutes and then at 80°C for 2 minutes. Then cool the plate to 4°C on the temperature module.
Pool all barcoded samples, noting the total volume.
Resuspend the AMPure XP Beads (AXP, or SPRI) by vortexing.
To the entire pooled barcoded sample from Step 4, add an equal volume of resuspended AMPure XP Beads (AXP, or SPRI).
Mix by pipetting for 5 minutes at room temperature.
materials_raw: Opentrons 96 Well Aluminum Block with Bio-Rad Well Plate 200 µL
Opentrons 24 Tube Rack with Eppendorf 1.5 mL Safe-Lock Snapcap
Opentrons 24 Well Aluminum Block with NEST 1.5 mL Screwcap
Opentrons P300 Single Channel Electronic Pipette (GEN2)
Opentrons P20 Single Channel Electronic Pipette (GEN2)
